questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
questions.answer: For property maintenance activities, which include the use of leaf blowers, the permitted hours are between 9:00 a.m. and 6:00 p.m. on weekdays, 9:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. on Saturdays, and 10:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. on Sundays only for property owners working on their own lot. The use of such equipment is not allowed on holidays.

questions.question: What are the penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations?
questions.answer: Any person violating any provision of this chapter, which would include gas leaf blower regulations, shall be guilty of a violation and, upon conviction, may be fined up to $250. Each day the violation continues is considered a separate offense, each punishable by a similar fine. Additionally, the Village may pursue legal action to enforce compliance or restrain violations.

questions.question: Are there any exceptions for school/government/commercial properties?
questions.answer: Yes, there are exceptions for certain activities related to schools, government, and commercial properties. Sounds connected with sporting events of any public or private school or with Village-sponsored or permitted special events or sports programs are exempt. Governmental agencies using public warning devices are also exempt. Additionally, public utilities and sounds created in the performance of any emergency work or action necessary to deliver essential services are exempt from the noise limitations. Owners of commercial properties may request to extend property maintenance hours under specific conditions and with Board of Trustees approval.

questions.question: How are violations reported and enforced?
questions.answer: Violations of the noise regulations are enforced by the Police Department, a Code Enforcement Officer, or other officials designated by the Village Board. A noise disturbance can be reported and substantiated in three ways: through sworn affidavits from two or more unrelated persons from different residences who agree on the noise details, through direct observation by an officer or official, or by measuring noise levels that exceed permissible limits using a sound level device.
